title: Pavan Jawanda
content:
People.ContactEmail pjawanda@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2014
- University of British Columbia
Pavan* is a Partner in our Business Law Group and also co-leads our U.S. Markets group. Pavan’s practice focuses on advising public and private companies and private equity firms on domestic and cross-border mergers and acquisitions, capital markets and securities, corporate finance, venture capital and other corporate and commercial transactions. He represents clients in various industries, including technology, private equity, gaming and casinos, financial services, infrastructure, energy and mining, as well as in Indigenous economic development. He has been recognized nationally in Canada as a leading lawyer in his field in Chambers Canada , Lexpert’s Canadian Legal Directory , Lexpert Special Edition – Technology , Lexpert Special Edition –Global Mining, Lexpert Special Edition – Finance and M&A, Best Lawyers in Canada and Thomson Reuters Stand-Out Lawyers as well as one of Canada’s leading lawyers under 40 in Lexpert Rising Stars: Canada’s Leading Lawyers Under 40 .
Prior to joining McCarthy Tétrault, Pavan was a corporate lawyer with Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP, one of New York’s leading international law firms, where he was based in its New York office from 2008 to 2012 and its Hong Kong office from 2012 to 2014, while also spending considerable time working with its Silicon Valley Menlo Park office. Pavan is also a U.S. Certified Public Accountant (CPA) and was previously an auditor with KPMG LLP.

#### Pavan’s experience includes:
- Advising Canadian, U.S. and international acquirers, targets, private equity firms and financial advisors on Canadian mergers and acquisitions, including public, private and cross-border M&A, private equity transactions and leveraged buyouts. He has advised various key global strategics on their cross-border and inbound Canadian tech acquisitions and corporate investments, including Intel, AMD, NVIDIA, NTT Data, PwC, Jabil, Clarivate, Edwards Lifesciences, Gates Foundation, Alibaba and Tencent. He has also advised key U.S. and global private equity and asset management firms on their cross-border Canadian transactions, including CVC, 26North, Brookfield, HG Vora, Fortress, Antarctica Capital, Morgan Stanley, Turn/River Capital and Brentwood Associates.
- Advising issuers, underwriters and private equity sponsors on domestic, U.S. and cross-border securities offerings of equity and debt, including initial public offerings, follow-on offerings, private placements, acquisition financings, high-yield debt offerings and consent solicitations. He has an extensive debt capital markets practice for issuers and dealers, having advised on over 75 bond offerings with an aggregate value exceeding $25 billion.
- Advising on corporate venture capital and strategic investments.
- Advising on Indigenous economic development transactions for First Nations communities.
Pavan has advised on corporate transactions originating in over 25 countries across a number of continents, with a particular focus on Canada, the U.S., China, India and other parts of Asia. Landmark transactions he has previously advised on have included the largest ever public equity offering by a U.S. utility company (selected "Americas Equity Issue of the Year" by International Financing Review ), the largest ever public equity offering by a U.S. oil and gas exploration and production company, the largest ever acquisition of a Canadian e-commerce company, the largest ever public-private partnership project in the U.S., one of the largest ever private equity acquisitions in India and one of the largest ever leveraged buyouts in the Philippines.
Pavan received his law degree from the University of British Columbia in 2008, where he was the recipient of a number of awards including the Blakes Scholar Award, Law Foundation of BC Award, Peter Howard Memorial Award, Law Foundation of BC Scholarship, Graduating Class of Law ‘53 Scholarship and prizes in corporate law, corporate transactions, real estate transactions and torts. He is called to the bars of British Columbia and New York. He is a committee member of the M&A Trends and Private Equity/Venture Capital committees of the American Bar Association and is a working group member of the ABA Canadian Private Target M&A Deal Points Study. He is also the co-editor of the Annotated British Columbia Securities Legislation book published by LexisNexis.
Pavan is also an Adjunct Professor at the University of British Columbia, Allard School of Law where he has taught accounting and finance for lawyers, has been a co-author of a number of articles in the areas of securities law and capital markets for the Practising Law Institute, has been a national lecturer in the areas of accounting and finance for lawyers as well as M&A transactions for the Canadian Bar Association and was previously the vice chair on the board of directors of The Learning Disabilities Association, a non-profit organization focused on supporting children with learning disabilities.
*denotes Law Corporation
